name: Lowest Common Ancestor of a Binary Search Tree
description: Given a BST, find the lowest common ancestor (LCA) of two given nodes p and q. The LCA is the lowest node that has both p and q as descendants (a node can be a descendant of itself).
constraints: The number of nodes is in [2, 10^5]. -10^9 <= Node.val <= 10^9. All values are unique. p != q. p and q exist in the BST.

performance.stress_intent: exercise ancestor handling and BST branch decisions; skewed trees make linear scans acceptable under the posted constraints
